            a = b;
            b = sum;
            idx += len_sum;
        }
        if (valid && idx == num.size() && fib.size() >= 3) {
            return fib;
        }
    }
}

Now, code:

But in C++, the code for the splitIntoFibonacci function would be:

vector<int> splitIntoFibonacci(string num) {
    for (int len1 = 1; len1 <= 10 && len1 < num.size(); ++len1) {
        if (len1 > num.size() - 1) break; // need at least one more digit for len2
        string s1 = num.substr(0, len1);
        if (s1.size() > 1 && s1[0] == '0') continue;
        long a = stol(s1);
        if (a > INT_MAX) continue;

        for (int len2 = 1; len2 <= 10 && len1 + len2 < num.size(); ++len2) {
            string s2 = num.substr(len1, len2);
            if (s2.size() > 1 && s2[0] == '0') continue;
            long b = stol(s2);
            if (b > INT_MAX) continue;

            vector<int> fib;
            fib.push_back(a);
            fib.push_back(b);
            int idx = len1 + len2;
            bool valid = true;
            while (idx < num.size()) {
                long sum = a + b;
                if (sum > INT_MAX) {
                    valid = false;
                    break;
                }
                string s_sum = to_string(sum);
                int len_sum = s_sum.size();
                // Check if the substring from idx of len_sum equals s_sum
                if (num.substr(idx, len_sum) != s_sum) {
                    valid = false;
                    break;
                }
                // Check leading zeros
                if (s_sum.size() > 1 && s_sum[0] == '0') {
                    valid = false;
                    break;
                }
                fib.push_back(sum);
                a = b;
                b = sum;
                idx += len_sum;
            }
            if (valid && idx == num.size() && fib.size() >= 3) {
                return fib;
            }
        }
    }
    return vector<int>();
}

So that after taking len1 and len2, there's at least one digit left. But during the while loop, the code may generate more numbers. Now, the code seems correct. Let's think about the time and space complexity.

Time complexity: For each possible len1 (up to 10), and len2 (up to 10), which gives O(1) possibilities (since 10 * 10 = 100). For each of these possibilities, the while loop processes the rest of the string. Each iteration of the while loop processes a part of the string. The total number of steps for each possibility is O(n), where n is the length of the string. So overall time complexity is O(n^2) in the worst case, but since len1 and len2 are bounded by 10, it's O(n * 10 * 10) = O(n), which is acceptable for n up to 200.

Space complexity: The space used is O(n) for the fib vector, which in the worst case could be O(n) (if the entire string is a valid sequence). But since each number is at least 1 digit, the maximum size is O(n). So space complexity is O(n).

Efficiency: HIGH, since the approach is efficient and leverages the constraints on the maximum length of the first two numbers.

Energy implications: LOW, since the algorithm is efficient and doesn't have high time or space complexity.

But in practice, for the code to be correct, we need to make sure that the loops are correctly bounded.

Another thing to note is that in C++, the stol function may throw an invalid_argument exception if the string is empty. However, in our code, len1 and len2 are at least 1, so the substrings are non-empty. So no problem here.

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    vector<int> splitIntoFibonacci(string num) {
        for (int len1 = 1; len1 <= 10 && len1 < num.size(); ++len1) {
            if (len1 > num.size() - 1) break;
            string s1 = num.substr(0, len1);
            if (s1.size() > 1 && s1[0] == '0') continue;
            long a = stol(s1);
            if (a > INT_MAX) continue;

            for (int len2 = 1; len2 <= 10 && len1 + len2 < num.size(); ++len2) {
                string s2 = num.substr(len1, len2);
                if (s2.size() > 1 && s2[0] == '0') continue;
                long b = stol(s2);
                if (b > INT_MAX) continue;

                vector<int> fib;
                fib.push_back(a);
                fib.push_back(b);
                int idx = len1 + len2;
                bool valid = true;
                while (idx < num.size()) {
                    long sum = a + b;
                    if (sum > INT_MAX) {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                    string s_sum = to_string(sum);
                    int len_sum = s_sum.size();
                    if (num.substr(idx, len_sum) != s_sum) {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                    if (s_sum.size() > 1 && s_sum[0] == '0') {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                    fib.push_back(sum);
                    a = b;
                    b = sum;
                    idx += len_sum;
                }
                if (valid && idx == num.size() && fib.size() >= 3) {
                    return fib;
                }
            }
        }
        return vector<int>();
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
